items.title: Five companies get regulator's approval for public issues
items.summary: Indian regulator Sebi has given the green light for five companies to launch their Initial Public Offerings. Dhoot Transmission, Horizon Industrial Parks, Hotel Polo Towers, Crystal Crop Protection, and Surgiwear have received Sebi's observations. This allows them to proceed with raising funds through public issues.

items.title: To exercise ESOPs, staff of listed cos can pledge shares in trading window closures
items.summary: Employees exercising stock options can now pledge shares for funds during trading window closures, thanks to a Sebi clarification. This provides regulatory comfort for bona fide transactions, aiding employees who rely on financing to exercise vested options. However, lenders invoking pledged shares will still face contra-trade restrictions.

items.title: SBI Q4 profit rises 6%, investors cautious over bond yield impact
items.summary: State Bank of India achieved its second-highest annual corporate profit, reaching ₹83,299 crore. Despite robust loan growth, investor sentiment dipped due to margin pressures and treasury losses. The bank's net profit rose 6% in the fourth quarter. Chairman CS Setty expressed confidence in continued credit growth for the current fiscal year.

items.title: Three pharma stocks where FIIs raised stake in the March quarter
items.summary: Pharma stocks have been attracting FIIs. Here are three stocks where FIIs have hiked stake in the March 2026 quarter.

items.summary: The closer your bond with your chatbot, the rustier your people-skills become

items.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.

items.title: Amar Bose: the man who heard what others missed
items.summary: Disappointed by a stereo system that failed Beethoven, Amar Bose reimagined sound itself—building a company that changed how the world listens to music and silence.

items.title: Tehran could withstand blockade for four months, CIA report shows, as fighting flares
items.summary: U.S. and Iran's efforts to end their conflict stall amid renewed clashes in the Gulf. A U.S. intelligence assessment suggests Iran can withstand a naval blockade for months, while Tehran accuses Washington of breaching a ceasefire. The U.S. also imposed new sanctions on individuals and companies aiding Iran's military.

items.title: Tata Consumer reshapes FMCG playbook around quick commerce, innovation and wellness
items.summary: Tata Consumer Products reported strong FY26 earnings, with revenue crossing ₹20,000 crore for the first time and profits and margins improving sharply

items.title: Accor raises its India play as domestic luxury demand reshapes hotels
items.summary: Accor India's chairman Gaurav Bhushan said that the luxury hotel sector has significant growth potential. With a focus on domestic travellers, Accor plans to expand its portfolio to 300 hotels by 2030, capitalizing on the increasing demand for high-quality hospitality experiences.

items.title: Britannia moves manufacturing to India amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: Britannia is also planning selective price hikes and grammage adjustments to offset rising fuel and packaging costs driven by geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Retail chains like Reliance Retail, DMart go on store expansion spree as demand recovers
items.summary: India's top retailers added 2,182 stores in FY26, the highest in three years, driven by rising demand, tax cuts, and growing consumer preference for organised retail in smaller towns.

items.title: Govt land transfer code set up to pare disputes, boost monetisation
items.summary: The Centre has introduced a nationwide framework for central government land transfers to curb disputes and boost asset monetisation. This new policy mandates separate valuations for buildings and clear guidelines for public versus commercial use, aiming to enhance transparency and unlock revenue for public projects. Transfers to private entities and states will now require cabinet approval.

items.title: Urban mobility is key to governance and ease of living, says CAG Murthy
items.summary: At the 5th BRICS Supreme Audit Institutions Summit, delegates from nine countries discussed challenges such as congestion, rapid urbanisation and environmental stress, while highlighting sustainable transport, digital governance and data-driven audit systems

items.title: SEBI plans major changes to buyback rules, seeks public comments
items.summary: The regulator also suggested simplifying compliance norms, removing the mandatory appointment of merchant bankers, improving shareholder communication and ensuring companies do not violate minimum public shareholding rules

items.title: Qualcomm bets on custom AI chips, targets Nvidia-led data centre market
items.summary: Qualcomm is entering the competitive data center AI chip market. The company plans to launch unique CPU chips for agentic AI and AI accelerators. Custom chips for hyperscalers will ship by year-end. Qualcomm also diversifies into AI wearables, automotive, and telecom infrastructure. The company sees significant growth potential in these areas.

items.summary: CANADA-STOCKS/ (UPDATE 2):CANADA STOCKS-TSX notches weekly gain as corporate profits impress investors

items.title: Treasuries Gain to End Choppy Week as Focus Shifts to Inflation
items.summary: Treasuries advanced at the close of the week as mixed economic data reinforced expectations the Federal Reserve will stay on hold, shifting the market’s focus back to inflation.

items.summary: GLOBAL-GRAINS/ (UPDATE 4):GRAINS-Soy turns higher on hopes of Chinese purchases; grains choppy

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500 and Nasdaq notch records, boosted by AI and earnings optimism
items.summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq reached record highs, driven by AI-related stocks like Nvidia and Sandisk, alongside a resilient jobs report. Despite broad sector declines, strong first-quarter earnings, particularly from tech giants, fueled investor optimism. This surge occurred despite rising oil prices and Middle East tensions, highlighting the market's focus on corporate performance and economic strength.

items.title: US stocks today: Applied Aerospace & Defense reveals 25% revenue jump in US IPO filing
items.summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense, a government contractor backed by private equity, has filed for a U.S. IPO after seeing its revenue jump 24.8% to $498.8 million in 2025. Despite a net loss of $17 million, the company's strong growth and its role in supplying critical defense hardware are drawing attention.

items.title: Air India delays salary hikes, pushes cost cuts amid volatile FY26 outlook
items.summary: Air India is tightening its belt. Facing global challenges, the airline is cutting costs. This move comes after significant expansion. Management aims to save money without harming service improvements. Salary raises are delayed. The airline is optimising routes and improving on-time performance.

items.title: Britannia bets on ‘Many Indias’ strategy amid inflation, shifting consumer demand
items.summary: Britannia is adopting a 'Many Indias' strategy. Startup-style teams will gain local decision-making power. This move aims to boost agility and product customisation for regional markets. The company is also investing in premium products and new food categories. These changes are expected to yield results in the coming quarters.

items.title: Hyundai India plans ₹7,500-cr capex for FY27 as Q4 net falls
items.summary: Hyundai India is investing ₹7,500 crore and launching two new models this fiscal year to capitalize on rising demand. The company plans to expand its Pune facility, boosting annual capacity to 1.14 million units by 2030. Despite a profit dip in Q4 FY26, domestic sales and exports showed strong growth, with record rural penetration and CNG contributions.

items.title: Beyond the Boundary: AI, influencers rewrite the cricket playbook
items.summary: Cricket in India is becoming more than just a sport. Broadcasters and streaming services are creating new shows and content around cricketers. This aims to keep fans engaged throughout the year. Platforms are partnering with teams and players for original programming. This shift creates new opportunities for brands to connect with audiences.

items.title: US yields fall after jobs report tops expectations; Iran ceasefire eyed
items.summary: USA-BONDS/ (UPDATE 1, GRAPHICS):TREASURIES-US yields fall after jobs report tops expectations; Iran ceasefire eyed

items.title: Manufacturing retains growth pace in Q4 despite cost pressures: Ficci
items.summary: India's manufacturing sector saw positive growth in Q4 FY26, driven by robust domestic demand. Despite rising input costs and geopolitical concerns, production levels increased, and manufacturers remain optimistic about future investments and hiring. Export sentiment also improved, with most firms reporting sufficient funding availability.

items.title: US stocks today: Anthropic signs $1.8 billion AI cloud deal with Akamai
items.summary: Artificial intelligence startup Anthropic has secured a significant computing deal worth $1.8 billion with Akamai Technologies. This agreement addresses the growing demand for Anthropic's AI software. Akamai's stock saw a substantial rise following the announcement of a long-term cloud deal with a major AI partner. The company is well-positioned to access necessary components for AI development.

items.title: States told to shift to new base year for GSDP by end of FY27
items.summary: The statistics ministry has directed states to adopt 2022-23 as the new base year for Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) computation by fiscal 2027. This move aligns state-level estimates with the revised national GDP framework, enhancing regional economic data accuracy. While states are also free to develop sub-state CPI, there's no central directive for this.

items.title: Oil PSUs suffer ₹30,000 cr under-recoveries a month
items.summary: Indian oil companies face significant monthly losses on petrol, diesel, and LPG.  This is due to selling fuels below international rates, which have risen sharply.  While some fuel prices have increased, regular petrol and diesel prices remain unchanged.  This situation is impacting the earnings of Indian Oil, HPCL, and BPCL, with their shares seeing a decline.

items.title: India orders antitrust probe into liquor giant Pernod's dealing with retailers
items.summary: India's competition watchdog has launched a probe into French liquor giant Pernod Ricard. Allegations suggest exclusive deals with retailers in New Delhi aimed to boost its brands. The Competition Commission of India found merit in claims of financial support to retailers in exchange for stocking Pernod products. This action could distort demand and limit consumer choice.

items.title: Former Wachtell lawyer in insider trading case later worked at investment bank, sources say
items.summary: USA-INSIDERTRADING/LAW FIRMS-CRIME (PIX):Former Wachtell lawyer in insider trading case later worked at investment bank, sources say

items.title: Musk, Altman Management Styles Come Under Fire at OpenAI Trial
items.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman both took a beating over their leadership styles in court testimony this week as a jury waded further into their OpenAI feud.

items.title: US market today: Biotech firm Odyssey hits nearly $900 million valuation as shares rise in Nasdaq debut
items.summary: Odyssey Therapeutics debuted on Nasdaq with an 11.1% surge, reaching a $899.9 million valuation. The autoimmune disease specialist successfully raised $279 million in its IPO, exceeding its target. This strong performance signals a revitalized biotech IPO market, with investors showing renewed confidence. Founder Dr. Gary D. Glick's prior success further bolsters investor appeal.

items.title: US market today: Strong US jobs data complicates any Kevin Warsh push for lower rates
items.summary: Strong U.S. hiring data in April has diminished the likelihood of Federal Reserve interest rate cuts this year, bolstering the stance of officials concerned about rising inflation. The robust job market, coupled with surging energy prices, suggests the Fed may need to maintain current rates for an extended period, impacting prospective Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's policy goals.

items.title: Jane Street rakes in record first-quarter trading haul of $16.1 billion, sources say
items.summary: Jane Street achieved a record $16.1 billion in trading revenue for the first quarter, driven by market volatility and gains in AI company stakes. The firm more than doubled profits to $10.3 billion, solidifying its lead in high-frequency trading. Medium-frequency strategies and investments in AI firms like Anthropic and CoreWeave significantly boosted performance.

items.title: Drug regulator cracks down on delayed safety reporting by pharma companies
items.summary: India's drug regulator CDSCO has issued new rules for drug safety reporting. Companies must now submit safety reports from the actual launch date of new drugs, not from when they were approved. This change aims to capture real-world safety data more effectively. Additionally, all forms and uses of a new drug will be covered under one safety report.

items.title: Fuel price hike on the cards as India struggles to absorb soaring crude costs
items.summary: India is grappling with significant economic challenges due to elevated global fuel prices. The government has absorbed substantial daily losses to shield consumers. However, the ongoing West Asia crisis and disruptions to energy supply routes are making this unsustainable. Tough decisions loom regarding fuel price adjustments to manage the financial burden and ensure energy security for the nation.

items.title: EQT emerges sole bidder for OmniActive Health Technologies
items.summary: Swedish investment powerhouse EQT is now leading the charge as the exclusive bidder for OmniActive Health Technologies, following the rejection of a competing offer from Temasek and Novo Holdings. Known for its robust presence in the nutraceutical industry, OmniActive is under the umbrella of TA Associates and has a strong export footprint in the United States.

items.title: Oman ports provide lifeline to Indian exporters, GCC nations
items.summary: Oman's strategically located Indian Ocean ports are facilitating vital food exports from India to six GCC nations, circumventing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Sohar and Salalah ports, equipped with specialized agricultural terminals and advanced infrastructure, are ensuring a steady supply of food to the Gulf, which relies heavily on imports.

items.title: Mother's Day 2026: Top 8 ways to build a safe investment portfolio for your mother's future
items.summary: In a special discussion on Zee Business, Prableen Bajpai, Founder of FinFix, and Pooja Bhinde, Certified Financial Planner, explained how a well-structured portfolio can mirror a mother’s role in safeguarding a family through all circumstances. Investors can gift a well-structured “mother-like” portfolio to their mothers this Mother’s Day, helping ensure their financial security.

items.title: Oil marketing companies lose  ₹30,000 crore a month amid high crude prices: Govt
items.summary: Pump price of petrol is currently at  ₹94.77 per litre in Delhi, while diesel is being retailed at at  ₹87.67 per litre. The domestic LPG cylinder is priced at  ₹913. The government last revised retail prices of petrol and diesel in 2024, cutting them by  ₹2 a litre ahead of the national elections.&nbsp;

items.title: Nvidia stock hits fresh record high as rally extends to third day, on track for biggest weekly gain in 6 months
items.summary: Nvidia's shares rose 3% to a record high of $217.80, marking its largest weekly gain since October. The surge follows a partnership with IREN to enhance AI infrastructure, involving a $2.1 billion investment and a five-year cloud services agreement worth $3.4 billion.

items.title: Balkrishna Dividend: Largecap auto ancillary firm declares 200% payout—Have it in portfolio?
items.summary: Balkrishna Industries (BALKRISIND) Dividend News: Auto parts maker Balkrishna Industries declared a final dividend of Rs 4 per equity share. Read on to learn about the payout in detail.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold jumps $50/oz, silver rallies $2 as traders track US-Iran talks
items.summary: Precious metals continued their upward trend, driven by optimism over a US-Iran peace deal and a weaker dollar. Gold rose $50 to $4,760, while silver surged to $82.16. A strong US jobs report indicated economic resilience, although concerns over inflation persist.

items.title: Is inflation eating into your returns? Experts share key portfolio strategy changes
items.summary: Experts Pankaj Mathpal, Managing Director of Optima Money, and Prathiba Girish, Founder of Finwise, explained that the slowdown in returns is not unusual, but rather part of a broader economic cycle influenced by inflation, currency weakness, and global uncertainty. In a discussion on Zee Business, both experts shared key insights on what investors should do.

items.title: PMJJBY disbursed Rs 17,600 crore to families since launch in 2015: LIC
items.summary: The Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana is vital for the government's 'Insurance for All by 2047' goal. Launched in 2015, it offers life cover of Rs 2 lakh for an annual premium of Rs 436. The scheme has enrolled over 27 crore individuals and disbursed Rs 17,600 crore in claims.

items.title: Labour Ministry notifies final rules for the Wage Code & Industrial Relations Code
items.summary: With Friday’s notification, the government has paved the way for the fixing of floor wages in India under the Code on Wages and setting up a reskilling fund for workers under the Industrial Relations Code along with the appointment of conciliation, certifying officer and appellate authority.

items.title: India seeks early resolution of issues related to implementation of EFTA trade pact
items.summary: In a bid to overcome obstacles in the trade pact with the EFTA bloc, India is pushing for quick resolutions. Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal engaged with Swiss officials to enhance the implementation of the India-EFTA Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement. Numerous new Indian products are now making their mark in the Swiss market.

items.summary: Lower provisions and steady loan growth support SBI earnings amid margin compression

items.title: Biocon shifts focus to margins, capacity utilisation after expansion phase
items.summary: Biocon is shifting its strategy to focus on capacity utilization, margin expansion, and improved returns after significant investments in global capabilities. The company reported a 13% revenue increase for FY26, though net profit saw a substantial decline. Biocon is also prioritizing sustainable, profitable growth and exploring in-licensing opportunities for its biosimilars portfolio.

items.title: Britannia shifts facility from Oman to India, considers price hike
items.summary: Britannia Industries has relocated its North American export business from Oman to Mundra, Gujarat, to navigate West Asian crises. The company is also considering price increases, including reducing grammage and raising prices on larger packs, to counter a 20% surge in fuel and packaging costs due to geopolitical issues and supply chain disruptions.

items.title: Vodafone weighs stake transfer to boost India unit capital: Report
items.summary: Vodafone Group is exploring transferring a portion of its stake in Vodafone Idea to the Indian unit for treasury holding, potentially replacing a cash infusion. This move aims to bolster Vodafone Idea's capital, support debt raising efforts, and generate funds for government dues and growth initiatives.

items.summary: Chairman C S Setty said that the bank, which has the highest market share in the highly competitive affordable housing segment, has seen the average loan size increase to Rs 51 lakh now, compared to around Rs 35-40 lakh two years ago.

items.summary: Arisinfra Solutions reported a 39% rise in FY26 revenue to Rs 1,067.5 crore, while profit surged over ten-fold to Rs 60.3 crore. Strong growth in contract manufacturing and Developer-as-a-Service businesses, improved margins and positive cash flow strengthened the company’s financial position.

items.summary: Tata Starbucks sees strong long-term growth in India's coffee market. The company is expanding its presence with new Starbucks Reserve stores. India remains a fast-growing market for Starbucks, with plans for more outlets. The focus is on sustainable and profitable expansion. Coffee penetration in India is growing, offering significant opportunities.

items.title: How AI is changing the way people discover and buy products online
items.summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming online shopping.  It now influences how people find and compare products before visiting websites.  This means brands must make their product information easily understandable by AI.  Consumer trust in reviews and human opinions remains crucial.  India is seeing this gradual shift in shopping habits.  The future of AI commerce depends on robust transaction infrastructure.
